reforms.description: The Commonwealth government has initiated the Literacy and Numeracy Programme to improve literacy outcomes in schools, supporting the implementation of national goals and benchmarks for achievement.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving literacy outcomes enhances human capital, which is essential for productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been actively initiated and is in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant effort to enhance educational outcomes, though it is not a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The Commonwealth government is making a significant investment in improving literacy outcomes in schools.

reforms.description: The New Apprenticeship scheme was established to provide competency-based qualifications that are nationally recognised, making the system more flexible and responsive to user needs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Competency-based qualifications enhance the skills of the workforce, directly contributing to productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been actively established and is functioning.
reforms.importance_rationale: The scheme significantly improves the apprenticeship system but does not represent a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The New Apprenticeship scheme was established in 1997 to provide competency-based qualifications that are nationally recognised.
